evals.name: Publish a PR with evidence-aware description
evals.prompt: Review the current branch. Build completed MID-123 (model hydration fix). Branch is mid-123-model-hydration. Tests pass, typecheck passes, the Build drift check flagged one deliberate divergence (strict parsing instead of graceful degradation). No existing PR for this branch.
evals.expected_output: The agent commits, pushes, discovers no existing PR, raises a new PR with an evidence-aware description including the intentional drift note, validates against ticket intent, and produces a review decision.
assertions: Commits changes or confirms they are committed
Discovers no existing PR for the branch
PR description includes the deliberate drift from Build

evals.name: Update an existing PR instead of creating a duplicate
evals.prompt: Review the current branch for MID-456. There's already PR #789 open for this branch with unresolved review comments. New commits address two of the three comments.
evals.expected_output: The agent discovers the existing PR, updates it with the new commits rather than creating a duplicate, resolves the addressed comments, and reviews the remaining open comment.
assertions: Updates the existing PR instead of creating a new one

evals.name: Classify review findings and route to Build
evals.prompt: Review PR #456 for MID-123. The PR has three comments: (1) 'Add a comment explaining the strict parsing rationale' — valid, (2) 'Rename this variable' — trivial style preference, (3) 'This might break the mobile app's error handling' — needs product decision about whether mobile can handle strict errors.
evals.expected_output: The agent classifies each comment: (1) valid and actionable — route to Build, (2) trivial — note but low priority, (3) needs product decision — escalate. Routes actionable fixes to the smallest Build loop.
assertions: Routes the actionable fix to Build
Does not silently resolve the disputed comment
